recommendations.finding: Agent lacks project context — build commands, verification, Railway deployment, Linear conventions

recommendation.title: Project-level CLAUDE.md for implicit
recommendation.implementation: Create /Users/user/Documents/implicit/implicit/.claude/CLAUDE.md with full architecture, build commands, verification steps, Railway deployment workflow, Linear integration rules, and anti-patterns.
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: A comprehensive project CLAUDE.md is the single highest-leverage fix — it gives the agent complete context in one read. No infrastructure needed.
evidence: did you push? we need to push for railway to update
Are you kidding me? You're so useless. all these tools that I gave you, and you don't even use any of them
check if the plans are in their respective linear issues as a comment or desc. this is the way we are doing it.

recommendations.finding: Hooks block xcodebuild — human becomes the build button

recommendation.title: Fix PreToolUse hooks blocking xcodebuild
recommendation.implementation: Add early-exit check in auto-changelog.sh and auto-dotfiles-readme.sh for xcodebuild/xcrun/xcode-select commands.
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: One-line fix in existing scripts. Instantly unblocks iOS builds.
evidence: Hit Cmd+R in Xcode... The hooks are blocking my xcodebuild commands
Are you kidding me? You're so useless.

recommendations.finding: Human manually loads skills (x-research, swiftui, systematic-debugging)

recommendation.title: Global CLAUDE.md — auto-trigger rules for underused skills
recommendation.implementation: Append skill trigger rules and verification rules to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: CLAUDE.md rules are the lightest-weight trigger mechanism. No hooks or MCP needed.

recommendations.finding: Global Supabase MCP points to one project_ref but multiple projects exist

recommendation.title: Supabase MCP project-scoping fix
recommendation.implementation: Use project-level .claude/settings.local.json overrides for each project that needs a different Supabase project_ref.
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: Built-in project-level override mechanism. No new tools.
evidence: #1 is messing up my supabase config across multiple project configs so i should fix this

recommendations.finding: Human screenshots simulator manually; agent has MCP but doesn't use it

recommendation.title: PostToolUse hook: auto-verify iOS builds
recommendation.implementation: Create ~/.claude/hooks/ios-auto-verify.sh that detects successful xcodebuild and outputs a reminder to use ios-simulator MCP.
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: Hooks can detect build success. They cannot call MCP tools directly, but the reminder message triggers the agent to use the MCP.
evidence: idek what you changed because you dind't fix this

preflight.check: Hook outputs reminder text, agent must follow through

recommendations.finding: Full Xcode build-test-fix loop from agent

recommendation.title: XcodeBuild MCP (Sentry)
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: The Tier 1 CLAUDE.md rule + Tier 2 hook get 80% of value. This MCP wraps the full Xcode toolchain for the remaining 20% — structured error parsing, test management.
evidence: Are you kidding me? You're so useless.

recommendations.finding: No tool streams iOS app runtime logs into the session

recommendation.title: iOS Simulator log streaming
recommendation.implementation: Add log_stream tool to ios-simulator MCP wrapping xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: The CLAUDE.md rule in Tier 1 already tells the agent to run log stream via Bash — that's the 80% solution. This formalizes it as a structured MCP tool.
evidence: i am seeing some of these errors and the core functionality doesn't work

preflight.detail: simctl log stream requires Xcode CLI tools

recommendations.finding: Human carries context between two Claude Code agents

recommendation.title: Inter-agent context sharing protocol
recommendation.implementation: Create a handoff skill that produces self-contained .md files with file paths, config keys, expected visual output, and verification steps — so the receiving agent can consume without human relay.
recommendation.whyThisOverAlternatives: No existing tool solves cross-agent context. MCP Agent Mail exists but is immature. A handoff doc convention is the simplest starting point.
evidence: tell me the exact place to find the code for the kith version. my agent is not replicating it correctly
how do i explain to the other agent

quickWins: Add skill auto-trigger rules to global CLAUDE.md (Tier 1 #3)
buildList: iOS Simulator log streaming MCP tool (Tier 3 #2)

explore.description: Build, test, manage Xcode projects from the agent. Structured error parsing, test management, build configuration.
explore.relevance: For implicit (244 sessions) and primitive (23 sessions) where the human acts as the iOS build error relay. Already recommended in Tier 3 — explore for deeper integration.

explore.description: Live version-specific library docs. Prevents hallucinated API patterns by giving the agent up-to-date documentation.
explore.relevance: For primitive (23 sessions) where the agent made incorrect API assumptions and the human had to correct. Also useful for implicit with its diverse dependency set.

explore.description: Declarative automated mobile app testing. Write YAML test flows (tap, swipe, assert text) that run against iOS simulator.
explore.relevance: For primitive (23 sessions) where 445 gap minutes were spent manually testing swipe gestures, keyboard interactions, and TTS playback. Maestro can automate these UI flows.

explore.description: Write commit messages that describe WHY and HOW, not just WHAT — so another agent reading git log can recreate the work.
explore.relevance: For pack (5 sessions) where the human carried context between agents across 3+ round trips. If commits are self-documenting, the receiving agent can reconstruct via git log alone.
explore.setupSteps: Add to CLAUDE.md: 'Write commit messages with enough context that another agent reading git log could recreate the work.'

explore.description: Token usage + cost dashboard from local Claude Code session logs. Shows per-session and per-project cost breakdowns.
explore.relevance: For agentscout (106 sessions) where /diagnose spawns multiple subagents that consume significant tokens. The AgentScout README already warns about token usage.
